page: Amazon Web Services c7gd.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor using the ARM64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network performance is anchored by a 1 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations show average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1532.88 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1473.0. However, multi-core and parallel processing capabilities are limited, as evidenced by a weak stress-ng multi-core score of 3041.67 and a poor bzip3 multi-threaded decompression score of 17.67 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is mixed, registering an average 53.57 GB/sec for large block reads but dropping to a weak 20.54 GB/sec for large block writes. Cryptographic performance is also constrained, with a poor Passmark CPU encryption score of 272.58, despite hardware AES support.

From a resource density perspective, the c7gd.large offers a cost-efficient entry point for ARM64-based workloads requiring high-speed local NVMe storage. The inclusion of the 118 GB local SSD offsets the need for external block storage for temporary files. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and moderate single-thread performance, this instance is suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded batch processing, local caching, and development or testing of ARM-native applications. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases, multi-threaded high-performance computing, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated ARM64 vCPUs powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the server delivers a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1473.0, but weaker multi-core and memory write performance. The local NVMe storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for disk-heavy operations without requiring external volumes. This instance is best suited for lightweight ARM64 development, single-threaded batch processing, and basic web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.large is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 2 Graviton vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a 118 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for lightweight, storage-reliant workloads.

tagline: An ARM64-based compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and integrated high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight workloads.

4.0 GB of system memory and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Bundled 118 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
Average single-core performance with weaker multi-core scaling
Suitable for lightweight web serving, ARM64 development, and local caching
